draft.introduction: The wise see the same Brahman in a learned brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste. This verse demolishes social hierarchy at the ontological level.

translation.text: The wise see the same in a learned and humble brahmin, in a cow, an elephant, a dog, and an outcaste. Brahman is flawless and the same in all; therefore they are established in Brahman.
translation.original_text: vidyā-vināya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini | śuni caiva śvapāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ

translator_notes.note: This verse has been used both to support and to challenge the caste system. On one reading, it transcends caste by declaring all beings ontologically equal in Brahman. On another, it spiritualizes equality while leaving social hierarchy intact.
